premises: Every student in the class passed a test.
The teacher praised everyone who passed.
Not all students in the class were praised by the teacher.
conclusion: Some students passed a test but were not praised.
conclusion_back_translation: There exists some x such that x is a student in the class, and there exists some test t such that t is a test and x passed t, and x is not praised by the teacher.
conclusion_discrepancy: The original says 'some students passed a test but were not praised' without necessarily requiring they be students in the class, but the back-translation adds the constraint that x is a student in the class. This strengthens the existential claim by restricting it to class students specifically.

premise_results.original: Every student in the class passed a test.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is a student in the class, then there exists some test t such that t is a test and x passed t.

premise_results.original: The teacher praised everyone who passed.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if there exists some test t such that t is a test and x passed t, then x is praised by the teacher.

premise_results.original: Not all students in the class were praised by the teacher.
premise_results.back_translation: It is not the case that every x satisfies: if x is a student in the class, then x is praised by the teacher.

premises: Employees can take a vacation or work from home.
Managers who work from home must be available by phone.
No manager is available by phone during vacation.
Alice is a manager who is not available by phone.

premise_results.original: Employees can take a vacation or work from home.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is an employee, then x is on vacation or x works from home (or both).

premise_results.original: Managers who work from home must be available by phone.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is a manager and x works from home, then x is available by phone.

premise_results.original: No manager is available by phone during vacation.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is a manager and x is on vacation, then x is not available by phone.

premise_results.original: Alice is a manager who is not available by phone.
premise_results.back_translation: Alice is a manager and alice is not available by phone.

premises: All members of the board voted for the proposal or abstained.
No one who abstained is considered a supporter.
Anyone who voted for the proposal is a supporter.
Some members of the board are not supporters.
conclusion: Some members of the board abstained.
conclusion_back_translation: There exists at least one x such that x is a Member and x Abstained.

premise_results.original: All members of the board voted for the proposal or abstained.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is a Member, then x Voted or x Abstained (or both).

premise_results.original: No one who abstained is considered a supporter.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x Abstained, then x is not a Supporter.

premise_results.original: Anyone who voted for the proposal is a supporter.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x Voted, then x is a Supporter.

premise_results.original: Some members of the board are not supporters.
premise_results.back_translation: There exists at least one x such that x is a Member and x is not a Supporter.

premises: Every restaurant on Main Street serves Italian food or Chinese food.
No restaurant that serves Chinese food has a Michelin star.
Some restaurants on Main Street have a Michelin star.
All Michelin-starred restaurants are expensive.
conclusion: Some expensive restaurants on Main Street serve Italian food.
conclusion_back_translation: There exists at least one x such that x is a restaurant, x is expensive, x is on Main Street, and x serves Italian food.

premise_results.original: Every restaurant on Main Street serves Italian food or Chinese food.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is on Main Street and x is a restaurant, then x serves Italian food or x serves Chinese food (or both).

premise_results.original: No restaurant that serves Chinese food has a Michelin star.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is a restaurant and x serves Chinese food, then x does not have a Michelin star.

premise_results.original: Some restaurants on Main Street have a Michelin star.
premise_results.back_translation: There exists at least one x such that x is a restaurant, x is on Main Street, and x has a Michelin star.

premise_results.original: All Michelin-starred restaurants are expensive.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is a restaurant and x has a Michelin star, then x is expensive.
premise_results.discrepancy: Original says 'All Michelin-starred restaurants are expensive' applying to any Michelin-starred entity, but back-translation adds the condition 'x is a restaurant', restricting the domain to restaurants only. If a non-restaurant entity had a Michelin star, the original would still require it to be expensive, but the back-translation would not.

premises: All of the books on the top shelf are first editions.
Some of the books on the top shelf are signed by the author.
No signed first edition is worth less than $500.
Some books on the top shelf are worth less than $100.
conclusion: Some first editions on the top shelf are not signed.
conclusion_back_translation: There exists something that is on the top shelf, is a book, is a first edition, and is not signed.
conclusion_discrepancy: Original says 'some first editions on the top shelf are not signed' without requiring they be books, but back-translation adds 'is a book' as an additional condition. However, since Statement 0 establishes all top-shelf books are first editions, the original only requires being a first edition on the top shelf, while the back-translation strengthens this by also requiring the thing to be a book.

premise_results.original: All of the books on the top shelf are first editions.
premise_results.back_translation: For every thing, if it is on the top shelf and is a book, then it is a first edition.

premise_results.original: Some of the books on the top shelf are signed by the author.
premise_results.back_translation: There exists something that is on the top shelf, is a book, and is signed.
premise_results.discrepancy: Original specifies 'signed by the author' but back-translation only says 'is signed', dropping the 'by the author' qualifier

premise_results.original: No signed first edition is worth less than $500.
premise_results.back_translation: For every thing, if it is signed and is a first edition, then it is not worth less than 500.

premise_results.original: Some books on the top shelf are worth less than $100.
premise_results.back_translation: There exists something that is on the top shelf, is a book, and is worth less than 100.

premises: Anyone who has a driver's license can rent a car.
To get a driver's license, you must pass both a written test and a driving test.
Sam passed the written test.
Everyone who took driving lessons passed the driving test.
Sam did not take driving lessons.
conclusion_discrepancy: Original specifies renting 'a car' but back-translation just says 'can rent' without specifying what can be rented.

premise_results.original: Anyone who has a driver's license can rent a car.
premise_results.back_translation: For all x, if x has a driver's license, then x can rent.
premise_results.discrepancy: Original specifies renting 'a car' but back-translation just says 'can rent' without specifying what can be rented.

premise_results.original: To get a driver's license, you must pass both a written test and a driving test.
premise_results.back_translation: For all x, if x has a driver's license, then x passed the written test and x passed the driving test.

premise_results.original: Sam passed the written test.
premise_results.back_translation: Sam passed the written test.

premise_results.original: Everyone who took driving lessons passed the driving test.
premise_results.back_translation: For all x, if x took lessons, then x passed the driving test.
premise_results.discrepancy: Original says 'took driving lessons' but back-translation says 'took lessons', which is broader and could include any kind of lessons.

premise_results.original: Sam did not take driving lessons.
premise_results.back_translation: Sam did not take lessons.
premise_results.discrepancy: Original says 'did not take driving lessons' but back-translation says 'did not take lessons', which is broader and could mean Sam took no lessons of any kind.

premises: Every patient who shows symptoms is tested.
Some tested patients receive a positive result.
All patients with a positive result are quarantined.
No quarantined patient is allowed visitors.
John is a patient who is allowed visitors.

premise_results.original: Every patient who shows symptoms is tested.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is a patient and x shows symptoms, then x is tested.

premise_results.original: Some tested patients receive a positive result.
premise_results.back_translation: There exists some x such that x is tested, x is a patient, and x has a positive result.

premise_results.original: All patients with a positive result are quarantined.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is a patient and x has a positive result, then x is quarantined.

premise_results.original: No quarantined patient is allowed visitors.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is quarantined, then x is not allowed visitors.
premise_results.discrepancy: Original restricts the domain to quarantined patients (if x is quarantined AND a patient, then not allowed visitors), but the back-translation applies to any quarantined entity regardless of whether they are a patient.

premise_results.original: John is a patient who is allowed visitors.
premise_results.back_translation: John is a patient and John is allowed visitors.

premises: All seniors are eligible for the discount.
Students who are not seniors must show a valid ID.
Anyone with a valid ID or who is eligible for the discount can enter.
Maria is a student who can enter.
Maria is not eligible for the discount.

premise_results.original: All seniors are eligible for the discount.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is a senior, then x is eligible.

premise_results.original: Students who are not seniors must show a valid ID.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is a student and x is not a senior, then x shows a valid ID.

premise_results.original: Anyone with a valid ID or who is eligible for the discount can enter.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x has a valid ID or x is eligible, then x can enter.

premise_results.original: Maria is a student who can enter.
premise_results.back_translation: Maria is a student and Maria can enter.

premise_results.original: Maria is not eligible for the discount.
premise_results.back_translation: Maria is not eligible.

premises: The committee approved all projects that received funding.
Some projects were submitted but did not receive funding.
Every approved project must file a quarterly report.
Project X was submitted and must file a quarterly report.

premise_results.original: The committee approved all projects that received funding.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x received funding, then x is approved.
premise_results.discrepancy: The original specifies that the committee (a specific agent) approved the projects, implying an institutional action. The back-translation drops the agent ('the committee') and simply states the projects are approved, losing the actor/source of approval.

premise_results.original: Some projects were submitted but did not receive funding.
premise_results.back_translation: There exists some x such that x was submitted and x did not receive funding.

premise_results.original: Every approved project must file a quarterly report.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is approved, then x files a quarterly report.
premise_results.discrepancy: The original uses 'must file', indicating a normative obligation or requirement. The back-translation uses 'files', which is a simple factual statement, losing the deontic/obligatory nuance.

premise_results.original: Project X was submitted and must file a quarterly report.
premise_results.back_translation: Project X was submitted and project X files a quarterly report.
premise_results.discrepancy: The original says Project X 'must file' a quarterly report, indicating an obligation or requirement. The back-translation says Project X 'files' a quarterly report, which is a factual claim rather than a normative one, losing the deontic nuance.

premises: All cats are mammals.
Some mammals are pets.
Every pet owner loves their pet.
Tom owns a cat named Whiskers.

premise_results.original: All cats are mammals.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x, if x is a cat, then x is a mammal.

premise_results.original: Some mammals are pets.
premise_results.back_translation: There exists some x such that x is both a mammal and a pet.

premise_results.original: Every pet owner loves their pet.
premise_results.back_translation: For every x and every y, if x is an owner of y and y is a pet, then x loves y.

premise_results.original: Tom owns a cat named Whiskers.
premise_results.back_translation: Whiskers is a cat, and Tom is an owner of Whiskers.
premise_results.discrepancy: Original states the cat is 'named Whiskers', implying Whiskers is the name of the cat. The back-translation only states Whiskers is a cat and Tom owns Whiskers, dropping the 'named Whiskers' aspect (i.e., that Whiskers is the cat's name).
